data.content:
[implementation] Dispatch forensics module landed.

New files (4 modules + 1 unit test + 1 manual test + 1 banner helper):
- src/lib/ansi-red-banner.ts (~35 LOC) — pure ANSI red-banner
  builder, zero deps, shared by FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE and
  FOOLERY DISPATCH FORENSIC. Extracted to its own file because the
  resolver's transitive workflow imports broke vi.mock isolation in
  3+ existing tests.
- src/lib/dispatch-forensics-types.ts (~110 LOC) — DispatchForensicBoundary
  union (10 names), BeatSnapshot, ForensicCategory, CaptureContext.
- src/lib/dispatch-forensics-storage.ts (~125 LOC) — fs writer,
  memory writer for hermetic tests, snapshotPath() pure helper.
- src/lib/dispatch-forensics-classify.ts (~190 LOC) — diff-based
  classifier (concurrent_claim_detected, kno_half_transition_suspected,
  our_agent_double_claim_suspected, lease_terminated_unexpectedly,
  unknown_state_change), banner builder, FOOLERY DISPATCH FORENSIC
  marker, ClassifierSignals interface for caller-supplied facts the
  classifier cannot infer (agentClaimExitedNonZero,
  foolerInitiatedLeaseTerminate).
- src/lib/dispatch-forensics.ts (~265 LOC) — captureBeatSnapshot,
  runPostTurnForensics. Top-level try/catch ensures the take loop
  cannot crash even if dependencies are stubs that throw.
- src/lib/__tests__/dispatch-forensics.test.ts (~340 LOC, 15 tests)
  — hermetic, all dependencies injected. Covers all 5 classifier
  categories + capture happy path + capture-error path + writer
  failure resilience + signal short-circuits.
- src/lib/__manual_tests__/dispatch-forensics-fs.test.ts (~110 LOC,
  2 tests) — touches real fs, runs only via bun run test:manual,
  excluded from default suite per Hermetic Test Policy.

Modified files (refactor + wiring):
- src/lib/dispatch-pool-resolver.ts — banner ANSI codes moved to
  ansi-red-banner.ts; existing FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE behavior
  unchanged (still uses the same red box).
- src/lib/knots-lease-runtime.ts — captureBeatSnapshot('pre_lease')
  before createLease, captureBeatSnapshot('post_lease') after.
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-agent.ts —
  captureBeatSnapshot('pre_prompt_send') in finalizeClaim before
  prompt_delivered audit fires.
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-iteration.ts —
  captureBeatSnapshot('post_turn_success' | 'post_turn_failure') at
  the very top of handleTakeIterationClose.
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-follow-up.ts —
  captureBeatSnapshot('pre_followup') in sendFollowUpPrompt before
  the prompt is dispatched.

Boundaries WIRED (5 of 10): pre_lease, post_lease, pre_prompt_send,
pre_followup, post_turn_success, post_turn_failure. Remaining 4
(pre_prompt_build, post_prompt_ack, periodic, post_rollback) are
defined in the boundary type union and will be wired as follow-up
work. The 5 wired boundaries cover the maestro-ca91 incident's
missing data: pre_lease vs post_turn_failure step_history diff
answers "did another agent claim this beat?" definitively.

Snapshot writes are fire-and-forget (`void captureBeatSnapshot(...)`)
and the function body is wrapped in a top-level try/catch so they
cannot crash the take loop even when dependencies are vi.mock
stubs. fetchSnapshotData uses a 5s timeout and a defensive function
typeof guard so missing/undefined deps return a captureErrors entry
instead of throwing.

Banner format example (rendered in test stderr):

  ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
  ║  FOOLERY DISPATCH FORENSIC: concurrent_claim_detected on   ║
  ║  beat b                                                    ║
  ║    session      = ses-1                                    ║
  ║    beat         = b                                        ║
  ║    iteration    = 1                                        ║
  ║    lease        = lease-A                                  ║
  ║    preSnapshot  = /p/pre.json                              ║
  ║    postSnapshot = /p/post.json                             ║
  ║    reasoning:                                              ║
  ║      step_history gained an action step bound to lease     ║
  ║      other-lease (agent=OtherAgent/...); our lease was     ║
  ║      lease-A. Another agent claimed this beat between our  ║
  ║      pre_lease and post_turn snapshots.                    ║
  ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Quality gates (in worktree, deps installed):
- bunx tsc --noEmit → clean
- bun run lint → clean
- bun run test → 3056 passed, 11 skipped, 42 todo (was 3041 before;
  +15 new dispatch-forensics tests, no regressions)
- bun run test:manual src/lib/__manual_tests__/dispatch-forensics-fs.test.ts
  → 2 passed
- bun run build → clean

Code style limits respected:
- Largest new file: dispatch-forensics.ts at ~265 LOC (well under 500).
- Largest function: classifier helpers each <60 LOC (well under 100).
- Lines under 100 cols.
